title: oxalis glaucescens
intro: Oxalis glaucescens, also known as the 'Purple Wood Sorrel', is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of Oxalis glaucescens include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Oxalis glaucescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil, and it is often used in traditional herbal remedies, particularly in the form of a poultice or salve.
